title: Thundering herd and backoff strategy
description: Synchronized retries amplify outages; full jitter spreads load so a recovering dependency is not re-overwhelmed.
body: Use when many clients retry the same failing dependency. Without jitter, exponential backoff still synchronizes retries into waves that re-knock-over a recovering service. Do not use fixed-interval retries or backoff without randomization on a hot dependency. Actions: apply full jitter (sleep = random(0, min(cap, base*2^attempt))), cap total attempts, and add a circuit breaker so a sustained failure stops retrying entirely. Validation signals include a load test showing retries spread across the window and the dependency recovering rather than flatlining. Risk boundary: a retry storm that takes down a shared dependency affects unrelated services and warrants immediate escalation.
